title: Agentic World Modeling: Foundations, Capabilities, Laws, and Beyond
summary: As AI systems move from generating text to accomplishing goals through sustained interaction, the ability to model environment dynamics becomes a central bottleneck. title: An Undecidability Proof for the Plan Existence Problem
summary: The plan existence problem asks, given a goal in the form of a formula in modal logic, an initial epistemic state (a pointed Kripke model), and a set of epistemic actions, whether there exists a sequence of actions that can be applied to reach the goal. We prove that even in the case where the preconditions of the epistemic actions have modal depth at most 1, and there are no postconditions, the plan existence problem is undecidable. The (un)decidability of this problem was previously unknown.

summary: Feature engineering for Electronic Health Records (EHR) is complicated by irregular observation intervals, variable measurement frequencies, and structural sparsity inherent to clinical time series. summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) can reason well, yet often miss decisive evidence when it is buried in long, noisy contexts. summary: Estimating racial disparity requires individual-level race data, which are often unavailable due to the sensitivity of collecting such information. summary: The Murali-Lakshmanan-Chua (MLC) circuit is a well-recognized prominent nonlinear, nonautonomous, and dissipative electronic circuit having a versatile chaotic nature. title: Generalized Invisibility in Metasurfaces

summary: This work focuses on the structure, wettability and corrosion behaviors of Ti-6Al-4V alloy after roughening treatments in different concentrations of NaOH aqueous solutions followed by low surface energy hexadecyltrimethoxysilane (HDTMS) coating. summary: This study experimentally validates a numerical model of electromagnetic propagation through the human head during the pronunciation of different vowels, with the goal of improving our understanding of the underlying physical phenomena. title: Brillouin-Enhanced Photonic Stepped-Frequency Radar
summary: Photonic stepped-frequency (SF) radar offers high range resolution and only requires low-speed driving electronics, but existing architectures face challenges in achieving low phase noise and uniform frequency steps simultaneously. summary: The ability to trap a single cell or microparticle in three dimensions is important for biomedical and microfluidic applications. summary: We present converged ab initio calculations of short-range neutrinoless double-beta ($0νββ$) decay nuclear matrix elements for the key experimental isotopes $^{76}$Ge, $^{82}$Se, $^{130}$Te and $^{136}$Xe. summary: We present an amplitude analysis of high-energy polarized photoproduction of $π^-Δ^{++}$ within a Regge exchange framework. title: Dense Matter and Compact Stars in Strong Magnetic Fields
summary: Compact stars serve as natural systems where matter exists at densities far beyond those achievable in laboratory experiments. title: The 0+-spectrum in rare earth nuclei within the pseudo-SU(3) shell model
summary: The study of the structure of the 0+ spectrum in heavy nuclei has drawn much attention in the last two decades. In this contribution we study their properties from a microscopic point of view. The pseudo-SU(3) model (\tilde{SU}(3)) is applied to some rare earth nuclei, namely to Sm, Gd, Dy, Er, Yb and Hf isotopes. title: Fusion of light nuclei in a multicluster realization of the three-body problem
summary: This work describes a few-body dynamics method based on the Faddeev integral equations in momentum space for determining the total cross sections of fusion and breakup reactions with two- and three-body final channels in the continuum, employing a cluster representation of the colliding nuclei. title: Confronting Color Glass Condensate at next-to-leading order with HERA data
summary: We perform a global analysis of HERA total inclusive cross section and charm quark production data to extract the non-perturbative initial condition for the next-to-leading order Balitsky-Kovchegov (BK) equation. title: The parity-violating asymmetry including QED corrections in high-energy electron-nucleus collisions
summary: The parity-violating asymmetry, accounting for the vector and axial-vector vertex plus self-energy correction as well as for vacuum polarization, is calculated nonperturbatively by solving the corresponding Dirac equation for the electronic scattering states. title: Hydrodynamics and Energy Correlators
summary: We study energy-energy correlators (EECs) in many-body quantum states, focusing on the matter produced in the aftermath of heavy-ion collisions. We analyze the angular structure of EECs in the collinear limit and identify a sequence of dynamical regimes. title: Gradient-Produced Neutrinos
summary: Sufficiently strong electric fields can produce charged-particle pairs via the Schwinger effect. We argue that steep matter-density gradients, as can arise in neutron star interiors, would analogously produce neutrino-antineutrino pairs. We then discuss observational signatures of these gradient-produced (anti)neutrinos and how they could provide new probes of neutron-star structure and baryon-dense QCD.

title: Quark and gluon production in the presence of the time-varying chiral magnetic current
summary: The chiral magnetic effect consists in the induction of the electric current along the direction of the magnetic field. title: Multi-Nucleon Transfer Reactions and the Creation and the Evolution of the Compound Nucleus
summary: There is no microscopic quantum approach based on the time-dependent Schrödinger equation which has yet to describe the formation of a compound nucleus. title: Ground-state properties of superheavy $Z=122$ isotopes within the deformed relativistic Hartree-Bogoliubov theory in continuum
summary: The ground-state properties of superheavy $Z = 122$ isotopes are investigated using the deformed relativistic Hartree-Bogoliubov theory in continuum (DRHBc). title: Dilepton Production as a Probe of Pion Condensation in Hot and Dense QCD Matter
summary: We investigate dilepton production from an isospin-asymmetric hot and dense medium in order to explore the role of isospin imbalance in electromagnetic spectral properties. title: Bayesian Inference of Dense-Matter Equations of State from Small-Radius Compact Stars with Twin-Star Scenarios
summary: We investigate dense-matter equations of state (EOSs) within a Bayesian framework, with particular emphasis on whether recent small-radius compact-star candidates can be accommodated in a twin-star scenario. title: Octupole correlation effects on two-neutron transfer intensity in rare-earth nuclei
summary: Impacts of octupole correlations on the low-lying $0^+$ states and two-neutron transfer intensities in rare-earth nuclei are investigated in terms of the interacting boson model that is based on the nuclear density functional theory. title: Conformal prediction for uncertainties in the neutron star equation of state
summary: We study uncertainties in the equation of state of neutron stars using conformal prediction as a distribution-free and model-agnostic method that provides coverage guarantees. title: Probing QCD instantons using jet correlation observables in proton-proton collisions at the LHC

title: Cutoff-independent predictions from nuclear lattice effective field theory
summary: Cutoff independence is an essential requirement for the predictive power of nuclear \textit{ab initio} calculations based on effective field theory (EFT). summary: In this work, we present a detailed comparison of the SuSAv2 (SuperScaling Approach version 2) and RDWIA (Relativistic Distorted-Wave Impulse Approximation) models with measurements of charged-current neutrino-induced single-pion production from different experiments (T2K, MINERvA and MiniBooNE), studying the differences between the two theoretical descriptions. summary: We use the two-flavor Linear Sigma Model with quarks as an effective description of QCD to investigate the nature of the chiral phase transition at finite baryon chemical potential and zero temperature. title: A Poincaré-covariant study of strange quark stars

summary: Bremsstrahlung emission of photons during nuclear reactions inside dense stellar medium is investigated in the paper. summary: We present a detailed systematics for comparing warped brane inflation with the observations, incorporating the effects of both moduli stabilization and ultraviolet bulk physics. summary: The blazar 3C 454.3 was revealed by the Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope to be in an exceptionally high flux state in July 2008. title: Biases and Uncertainties in Physical Parameter Estimates of Lyman Break Galaxies from Broad-band Photometry
summary: We investigate the biases and uncertainties in estimates of physical parameters of high-redshift Lyman break galaxies (LBGs), such as stellar mass, mean stellar population age, and star formation rate (SFR), obtained from broad-band photometry. summary: We investigate the effects of neutrino heating and alpha-particle recombination on the hydrodynamics of core-collapse supernovae. title: A Gamma-Ray Burst/Pulsar for Cosmic-Ray Positrons with a Dark Matter-like Spectrum
summary: We propose that a nearby gamma-ray burst (GRB) or GRB-like (old, single and short-lived) pulsar/supernova remnant/microquasar about 10^{5-6} years ago may be responsible for the excesses of cosmic-ray positrons and electrons recently observed by the PAMELA, ATIC/PPB-BETS, Fermi and HESS experiments. summary: We study the impact of catastrophic errors occurring in the photometric redshifts of galaxies on cosmological parameter estimates with cosmic shear tomography. title: Stellar Ages from Stellar Rotation

title: Source region of the 2003 November 18 CME that led to the strongest magnetic storm of cycle 23

summary: We recalibrate a standard solar model seismologically to estimate the main-sequence age of the Sun. title: Origin of Europa and the Galilean Satellites
summary: Europa is believed to have formed near the very end of Jupiter's own accretion, within a circumplanetary disk of gas and solid particles. title: Active Galactic Nuclei, Radio Jets and Acceleration of UHECRs
summary: We present the general properties of the Active Galactic Nuclei (AGNs) and discuss the origin and structure of jets that are associated to a fraction of these objects. We then we address the problems of particle acceleration at highly relativistic energies and set limits on the luminosity of AGN jets for being origin of UHECRs.

title: Compressed sensing imaging techniques for radio interferometry
summary: Radio interferometry probes astrophysical signals through incomplete and noisy Fourier measurements. The theory of compressed sensing demonstrates that such measurements may actually suffice for accurate reconstruction of sparse or compressible signals. title: The CHilean Automatic Supernova sEarch (CHASE)
summary: The CHASE project started in 2007 with the aim of providing young southern supernovae (SNe) to the Carnegie Supernova Project (CSP) and Millennium Center for Supernova Studies (MCSS) follow-up programs. So far CHASE has discovered 33 SNe with an average of more than 2.5 SNe per month in 2008. In addition to the search we are carrying out a follow-up program targeting bright SNe. title: Measuring interstellar magnetic fields by radio synchrotron emission
summary: Radio synchrotron emission, its polarization and its Faraday rotation are powerful tools to study the strength and structure of interstellar magnetic fields. The total intensity traces the strength and distribution of total magnetic fields. Total fields in gas-rich spiral arms and bars of nearby galaxies have strengths of 20-30 $μ$Gauss, due to the amplification of turbulent fields, and are dynamically important. title: Intermediate inflation on the brane
summary: Brane inflationary universe model in the context of intermediate inflation is studied. General conditions for this model to be realizable are discussed. In the high-energy limit we describe in great details the characteristic of this model.

title: Friedmann cosmology with bulk viscosity: a concrete model for dark energy
summary: The universe content is considered as a non-perfect fluid with bulk viscosity and can be described by a general equation of state (endowed some deviation from the conventionally assumed cosmic perfect fluid model). title: Detecting Solar Neutrino Flare in Megaton and km^3 detectors
summary: To foresee a solar flare neutrino signal we infer its upper and lower bound. The upper bound was derived since a few years by general energy equipartition arguments on observed solar particle flare. title: Anisotropic distribution functions for spherical galaxies
summary: A method is presented for finding anisotropic distribution functions for stellar systems with known, spherically symmetric, densities, which depends only on the two classical integrals of the energy and the magnitude of the angular momentum. title: Consistency of Equations in the Second-order Gauge-invariant Cosmological Perturbation Theory
summary: Along the general framework of the gauge-invariant perturbation theory developed in the papers [K. Nakamura, Prog. Theor. title: Quantum vacuum and accelerated expansion
summary: A new approach to extraction of quantum vacuum energy, in the context of the accelerated expansion, is proposed, and it is shown that experimentally realistic orders of values can be derived. The idea has been implemented in the framework of the Friedmann-Lemaitre-Robertson-Walker geometry in the language of the effective action in the relativistic formalism of Schwinger's proper time and Seeley-DeWitt's heat kernel expansion.

title: Near-IR spectroscopic ages of massive star clusters in M82
summary: Like other starburst galaxies, M82 hosts compact, massive young star clusters that are interesting both in their own right and as benchmarks for population synthesis models. summary: In this paper, we present the Electric Mobility Dial-a-Ride Problem (EM-DARP), which extends the Electric Vehicle Dial-a-Ride Problem (EV-DARP) to better accommodate human-focused mobility services. title: GCImOpt: Learning efficient goal-conditioned policies by imitating optimal trajectories
summary: Imitation learning is a well-established approach for machine-learning-based control. However, its applicability depends on having access to demonstrations, which are often expensive to collect and/or suboptimal for solving the task. In this work, we present GCImOpt, an approach to learn efficient goal-conditioned policies by training on datasets generated by trajectory optimization. title: Compositional Online Learning for Multi-Objective System Co-Design
summary: Many engineered systems must balance competing objectives, such as performance and safety, cost and reliability, or efficiency and sustainability, and are naturally modeled as compositions of interacting subsystems. title: Multi-robot obstacle-aware shepherding of non-cohesive target agents
summary: This paper presents a novel control strategy for multi-agent shepherding of non-cohesive targets in obstacle-rich environments. title: Strategically Robust Linear Quadratic Dynamic Games
summary: We study linear quadratic dynamic games where players are uncertain about each other's control policies or goals and consequently seek to be strategically robust. title: Control of Multi-agent Systems under STL Specifications based on Prescribed Performance Observers
summary: This paper addresses decentralized control of large-scale heterogeneous multi-agent systems subject to bounded external disturbances and limited communication, with the objective of satisfying cooperative Signal Temporal Logic (STL) specifications. title: Optimal sequential decision-making for error propagation mitigation in digital twins
summary: Here, we explore the problem of error propagation mitigation in modular digital twins as a sequential decision process. title: Sampling-Based Safety Filter with Probabilistic Restrictiveness Guarantee
summary: Ensuring safety is a critical requirement for autonomous systems, yet providing formal guarantees for nominal controllers remains a significant challenge. In this paper, we propose a modular sampling-based safety filter to ensure the safety of arbitrary nominal control inputs. title: Global Convergence of Policy Gradient Methods for ReLU Controllers in Linear Quadratic Regulation
summary: We study the convergence of model-based policy gradient for the deterministic, scalar, discounted linear-quadratic regulator when the controller is an overparameterized one-hidden-layer ReLU network without biases. title: Characterizing pitch and roll torque coupling in insect-sized flapping-wing robots using a microfabricated gimbal

title: A Hybrid Reinforcement and Self-Supervised Learning Aided Benders Decomposition Algorithm
summary: We propose a hybrid reinforcement and self-supervised learning framework for accelerating generalized Benders decomposition (GBD). title: Empirical Assessment of Time-Series Foundation Models For Power System Forecasting Applications
summary: Accurate forecasting of electric load and renewable generation is essential for reliable and cost effective power system operations. title: Model Predictive Control of Hybrid Dynamical Systems
summary: The problem of controlling hybrid dynamical systems using model predictive control (MPC) is formulated and sufficient conditions for asymptotic stability of a set are provided. summary: Discrete-event systems and supervisory control theory provide a rigorous framework for specifying correct-by-construction behavior. title: Resilience Revisited: A Multidimensional Framework Derived from Realistic Attack Scenarios
summary: Power systems are increasingly vulnerable to high-impact, low-probability (HILP) events, including coordinated cyberattacks targeting inverter-based resources. title: An Adaptive Kalman Filter that Learns the Coloring Dynamics of the Process Noise
summary: In many applications of state estimation, the process noise is colored; this case is addressed by applying the standard Kalman filter (KF) to dynamics that are augmented with the coloring dynamics. summary: This paper addresses distributed state estimation for multi-agent systems with local and relative measurements, motivated by cooperative localization problems in which the global state dimension scales with the size of the network. summary: This paper addresses characterizations of Integral Input-to-State Stability (iISS) for hybrid systems with memory. title: Using Assembly Language for Creating Games
summary: The aim of this paper is to demonstrate some interesting and useful approaches for writing a program in the assembly language. title: Encrypted Visual Feedback Control Using RLWE-Based Cryptosystem
summary: This study proposes an encrypted visual feedback control algorithm for regulating a one-dimensional stage using Ring Learning With Errors (RLWE) encryption. title: Estimation of Unknown Parameters in Presence of Perturbations and Noises with Application to GPEBO Design
summary: A problem of online estimation of unknown parameters is considered for a linear regression equation, which is affected by an additive perturbation that can be caused by measurement noise (that corrupts regressor and regressand), as well as external perturbations. summary: Distributed stochastic optimization enables multi-agent collaboration in applications such as distributed learning and sensor networks, but also raises critical privacy concerns due to the involvement of sensitive data. summary: We study sensor scheduling for continuous-discrete Kalman filtering with Poisson measurement arrivals and propose an information-form deterministic surrogate for scalable offline design. title: Analytical PI Tuning for Second-Order Plants with Monotonic Response and Minimum Settling Time
summary: Background: Tuning proportional-integral (PI) controllers for second-order plants to achieve monotonic step response with minimum settling time is an important problem in analytical control design. title: CLT-Optimal Parameter Error Bounds for Linear System Identification
summary: There has been remarkable progress over the past decade in establishing finite-sample, non-asymptotic bounds on recovering unknown system parameters from observed system behavior. title: Frequency Security Assessment in Power Systems With High Penetration of Renewables Considering Spatio-Temporal Frequency Distribution
summary: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources exacerbates the spatial and temporal differences in frequency across the power system, posing a serious challenge to the accurate and efficient assessment of system frequency security. summary: This paper proposes a scalable coordination framework with aggregator-side privacy protection for storage-like distributed energy resources (DERs).
